frameworks.name: Bayesian pedagogical sampling
frameworks.one_line: teachers choose examples that maximize a learner's posterior belief in the correct answer

frameworks.name: mentalizing / theory of mind
frameworks.one_line: social-brain regions represent the learner's evolving belief state during teaching

frameworks.name: computational model of communication
frameworks.one_line: teaching framed as selecting evidence under a model of the learner

core_question: What computations guide teachers' decisions about which examples to communicate to a learner?
key_assumptions: teachers maintain an internal model of the learner
example selection is goal-directed toward improving the learner's belief
mentalizing regions encode others' mental contents
manipulations: abstract multiple-choice questions for which teachers selected examples
fMRI scanning of teachers during example selection
independent learner sample tested on teacher-selected examples
key_findings: example choices best fit a model maximizing learner belief in the correct answer
teachers' predictions of learner performance tracked actual learner outcomes
bilateral TPJ and middle/dorsal medial prefrontal cortex tracked learner posterior belief
interpretations: teaching recruits mentalizing circuitry to represent learner beliefs
human pedagogy is supported by belief-directed example selection
